raw.title: The Tithe (Revolt Variant)

source_hook.title: The Tithe
source_hook.premise: A frontier settlement is short on its annual Resonant tithe. The Synod does not care why. If the tithe is not met, replacement children will be selected.
source_hook.crucible: The Synod will take children if the numbers are not met.

scenario_shape.premise: The tithe shortage has already become a public legitimacy crisis. Settlement leadership has fractured between compliance and resistance. The Warden arrives as Throne authority to restore order, but the settlement's factions are preparing to act—some to force compliance, some to hide children, some to negotiate exemption, and some to openly refuse the Synod's claim.
scenario_shape.why_this_run: This run foregrounds the political collapse that follows when institutional pressure becomes unbearable and visible. The Warden is not investigating a hidden problem or negotiating behind closed doors—they are walking into a settlement where everyone knows what is coming, and multiple factions are already mobilizing around incompatible solutions. The PC's authority is the last chance for managed resolution, or the spark that ignites open defiance.
scenario_shape.what_this_is_not: This is not a mystery about where the Resonants went, or a chamber play to negotiate exemptions with House or Synod elites. The shortage is known. The factions are public. The crisis is structural and visible.
scenario_shape.selection_rationale: Revolt mode was chosen because the creative angle specifies that the legitimacy crisis is already public, and the arc variant explicitly requests this scenario bias. This mode produces stronger player choice than investigation (the facts are known) or chamber_play (the factions are mobilizing beyond negotiation). Protection mode would script the PC into a defensive posture; revolt mode lets them enforce, mediate, defect, or witness collapse based on stance.
scenario_shape.rejected_default_shape: The default shape for this hook is investigation—tracking down missing Resonants, uncovering corruption, or discovering why the shortage occurred. That mode would focus on hidden information and gradual revelation. This run rejects that in favor of open factional conflict where the player must act under institutional and moral pressure with incomplete control.

raw.arc_question: Can the Warden hold the settlement together under the weight of an impossible mandate, or will they witness—or enable—the fracture of imperial authority on the frontier?
raw.core_crucible: The Synod's tithe ship arrives in five weeks. If the numbers are not met, supplementary selection will occur. Every faction knows this. No one can stop it through channels. The Warden's authority can enforce compliance, legitimize resistance, or shatter trying to do both.
invariant_facts: The settlement is short by four Resonants on its annual tithe.
The Synod does not negotiate tithe amounts; shortfalls trigger supplementary selection from the next cohort.
The tithe ship arrives in five weeks, filed under transit authority.
The Warden carries an imperial writ authorizing them to restore order and ensure compliance with Synod mandates.
Multiple factions within the settlement have already begun preparing conflicting responses to the shortage.
The Throne sent the Warden specifically because both House authority and Synod authority have failed to resolve the crisis.
variable_truths_for_this_run: The settlement's House-sworn administrator has lost control and is preparing to flee before the tithe ship arrives.
A group of parents has formed a hidden network to falsify records and hide children who test near the Resonant threshold.
The local Synod lector has requested armed support to enforce supplementary selection, escalating the situation from administrative to martial.
An Undrift contact within the settlement has offered to extract families, but only if the Warden looks the other way.
The shortage occurred because two young Resonants died in a poorly maintained attunement array; the House supervisor covered it up rather than report the loss.
One of the settlement's militia sergeants is preparing to seize the tithe records and destroy them, believing it will buy time.
A minor House envoy is en route, promising exemption in exchange for the settlement's loyalty transfer—an offer that would be considered heresy if formalized.

durable_forces.name: Synod Compliance Office (Sector Mandate)
durable_forces.agenda: Ensure the tithe is met on schedule; frame supplementary selection as moral necessity and institutional care.
durable_forces.leverage: Legal authority to conduct supplementary selection; the tithe ship and armed lectors; the weight of doctrine and a thousand years of precedent.
durable_forces.fear: That visible resistance will spread to other settlements; that the Throne will blame the Synod for frontier instability.
durable_forces.pressure_style: Righteous, procedural, and unyielding. Frames enforcement as duty and refusal as harm to the greater Hegemony. Uses administrative language to make violence sound inevitable.

durable_forces.name: The Parents' Movement (Settlement Factions)
durable_forces.agenda: Protect their children from supplementary selection by any means necessary—record falsification, hiding, or open refusal.
durable_forces.leverage: Local knowledge, popular legitimacy, willingness to act outside the law, ability to shelter and move people covertly.
durable_forces.fear: That their children will be taken; that the Warden will enforce compliance; that resistance will bring reprisal not just on them but on the entire settlement.
durable_forces.pressure_style: Desperate, organized, and morally certain. They speak in terms of family, survival, and betrayal. They test the Warden's willingness to enforce.

durable_forces.name: House Czerynn (Local Administration)
durable_forces.agenda: Avoid blame for the shortage and maintain House standing; restore order without losing allocation privileges or incurring Synod sanction.
durable_forces.leverage: Formal administrative control, records access, small garrison of sworn retainers, ability to delay or expedite bureaucratic action.
durable_forces.fear: That they will be blamed for the deaths that caused the shortage; that the settlement will become ungovernable; that a rival House will exploit the crisis.
durable_forces.pressure_style: Strategic, defensive, and slippery. House representatives speak formally and try to redirect responsibility. They offer cooperation but withhold commitment.

durable_forces.name: The Undrift (Frontier Network)
durable_forces.agenda: Extract families and children before the tithe ship arrives; expand the network of those who have escaped Synod control.
durable_forces.leverage: Hidden routes, falsified transit papers, off-record safe houses, and the promise of freedom outside the Hegemony's reach.
durable_forces.fear: Exposure and annihilation; that the Warden will prioritize duty over mercy; that extraction attempts will collapse the entire network.
durable_forces.pressure_style: Cautious, transactional, and morally urgent. They make no appeals to law or duty, only to what the Warden can live with. Every offer is a test.

durable_forces.name: The Throne (Institutional Expectation)
durable_forces.agenda: Restore stability without open revolt; preserve the balance between Synod authority and House legitimacy; avoid visible fracture.
durable_forces.leverage: The Warden's mandate and the weight of imperial duty; the implicit threat of escalation if the Warden fails.
durable_forces.fear: That the frontier will become ungovernable; that the Synod or a House will force the Throne to choose sides; that the Warden will defect or exceed authority.
durable_forces.pressure_style: The Throne is not a voiced presence but an institutional weight the Warden carries. Its pressure is felt in the writ, in the expectations of other factions, and in the cost of failure.

durable_npc_seeds.dramatic_function: Voice of institutional righteousness and procedural inevitability; escalates when resisted, frames enforcement as moral duty.
durable_npc_seeds.private_pressure: Brandlis believes deeply in the necessity of the tithe system, but fears being remembered as the lector who sparked a frontier revolt. He needs the Warden to legitimize enforcement.
durable_npc_seeds.reuse_guidance: Reappears whenever Synod authority is invoked or challenged. Becomes more rigid or more desperate depending on how the Warden's stance hardens. May request armed enforcement or attempt to conduct selection himself.

durable_npc_seeds.dramatic_function: Represents House authority in collapse; desperate to avoid blame, increasingly willing to abandon the settlement to protect herself.
durable_npc_seeds.private_pressure: Vey covered up the maintenance failure that killed two Resonants. She knows the shortage is her fault, and she will do anything to prevent that from becoming formal record.
durable_npc_seeds.reuse_guidance: Reappears in bureaucratic and crisis moments. May offer cooperation, bribe, sabotage, or flight depending on the Warden's approach. Her desperation is a contagious pressure.

durable_npc_seeds.dramatic_function: Moral anchor for the resistance; tests the Warden's willingness to enforce an unjust mandate; escalates to direct action if ignored.
durable_npc_seeds.private_pressure: Haake's daughter tested near-threshold last year. If supplementary selection occurs, she will be taken. Haake is willing to die to prevent it.
durable_npc_seeds.reuse_guidance: Reappears whenever the Parents' Movement acts. Haake's stance toward the Warden shifts based on respect, betrayal, or alliance. She can be an ally, an enemy, or a tragedy.

durable_npc_seeds.dramatic_function: Represents the enforcement arm caught between loyalty and conscience; capable of violence but seeking a reason not to use it.
durable_npc_seeds.private_pressure: Korell has sworn oaths to House Czerynn but lives among the families being threatened. He will follow the Warden's lead if it seems legitimate, but he will not fire on his neighbors without moral cover.
durable_npc_seeds.reuse_guidance: Reappears in confrontation and enforcement scenes. Korell's loyalty is the hinge—if the Warden gives him a legitimate path, he will hold the line. If the Warden's authority fractures, he will choose sides.

durable_npc_seeds.dramatic_function: Offers a way out for families; tests the Warden's willingness to look away; becomes visible only when the Warden shows an opening.
durable_npc_seeds.private_pressure: Senna was a House retainer before defecting. She knows the system intimately and despises it, but she also knows that one careless extraction attempt could burn the entire network.
durable_npc_seeds.reuse_guidance: Reappears when the Warden creates space for quiet defiance. Senna does not argue morality—she offers capability. She will vanish or act based on trust, not persuasion.

durable_npc_seeds.dramatic_function: Introduces a political escape hatch that would solve the immediate crisis but fracture loyalty structures; pressures the Warden to act as kingmaker.
durable_npc_seeds.private_pressure: Talric sees the crisis as an opportunity to pry the settlement away from House Czerynn. He has no interest in the children—only in the territory and the precedent.
durable_npc_seeds.reuse_guidance: Appears mid-arc when the crisis is visible to outside powers. Talric is polite, transactional, and patient. He offers a deal that would work—and poison everything after.

pressure_engines.name: The Tithe Ship Arrival Clock
pressure_engines.advances_when: Time passes; the Warden fails to produce a resolution; factions delay or sabotage compliance efforts; violence or bureaucratic collapse consumes time.
pressure_engines.slows_when: The Warden achieves temporary agreements; factions cooperate; House or Synod authority grants short delays; transit schedules are contested or rewritten.
pressure_engines.visible_symptoms: Public announcements of days remaining; lector's increasing urgency; parents' desperation intensifying; House officials preparing exit strategies; militia positioning around key sites.

pressure_engines.name: Institutional Legitimacy Erosion
pressure_engines.advances_when: The Warden enforces without moral clarity; visible injustices occur; factions openly defy authority; the Warden is seen taking sides; promises are broken; violence is used.
pressure_engines.slows_when: The Warden acts transparently and justly; oaths are witnessed and honored; small protections are delivered; the Warden visibly bears the cost of decisions.
pressure_engines.visible_symptoms: Settlement cooperation declines; militia becomes unreliable; House authority is ignored; Synod demands escalate; people stop speaking freely to the Warden; Undrift becomes bolder.

pressure_engines.name: Parents' Movement Escalation
pressure_engines.advances_when: The Warden is seen as complicit with the Synod; enforcement actions occur; families are threatened; Haake and others lose hope in negotiation; hidden networks grow bold.
pressure_engines.slows_when: The Warden protects families; shows visible resistance to Synod pressure; creates space for alternatives; earns trust through action; witnesses oaths of protection.
pressure_engines.visible_symptoms: Records are falsified or destroyed; children disappear; public assemblies become confrontational; militia faces non-cooperation; sabotage of settlement infrastructure; open defiance.

pressure_engines.name: Synod Enforcement Demand Clock
pressure_engines.advances_when: Resistance becomes visible; the lector feels undermined; the Warden is seen as insufficiently loyal; supplementary selection is obstructed; Synod fears loss of control.
pressure_engines.slows_when: The Warden enforces compliance; delivers visible cooperation; isolates resistance; provides the lector with bureaucratic cover; frames enforcement as joint action.
pressure_engines.visible_symptoms: Lector requests armed support; Synod reinforcements are summoned; lector begins preparing to conduct selection independently; doctrine is invoked more rigidly; heresy accusations surface.

pressure_engines.name: House Authority Collapse or Rival Exploitation
pressure_engines.advances_when: Administrator Vey's failure becomes visible; the crisis spreads beyond the settlement; rival Houses notice the instability; the Warden destabilizes House control.
pressure_engines.slows_when: The Warden stabilizes House legitimacy; protects Vey's position (or replaces her cleanly); prevents rival House interference; delivers a resolution that preserves allocation standing.
pressure_engines.visible_symptoms: Vey prepares to flee; rival House envoys arrive; allocation privileges are contested; settlement officials hedge their loyalty; records are altered or hidden.

player_stance_axes.axis: Enforce the Mandate vs. Protect the Families
signals_a: Cooperating openly with Lector Brandlis
Using intimidation or authority to compel compliance
Framing the tithe as necessary and just
Suppressing resistance or hidden networks
Invoking the Warden's writ and Throne duty
signals_b: Visibly resisting or delaying Synod demands
Helping families hide children or falsify records
Framing the tithe as unjust or unbearable
Refusing to enforce supplementary selection
Allying with Haake or the Parents' Movement
player_stance_axes.if_a_hardens: The Synod lector treats the Warden as an ally and expects full enforcement; parents see the Warden as complicit; militia may fracture or obey; Undrift networks vanish; the arc moves toward enforced compliance or violent suppression.
player_stance_axes.if_b_hardens: The Parents' Movement trusts the Warden and mobilizes openly; the Synod lector escalates and may summon reinforcement; House authority collapses; the arc moves toward defection, extraction, or open revolt.

signals_a: Speaking openly about the shortage's cause
Refusing to falsify records or hide information
Holding public oaths or assemblies
Forcing factions to state positions openly
Acting as a visible Throne presence
signals_b: Cutting secret deals with factions
Falsifying or burying records
Operating through intermediaries
Keeping factions ignorant of each other's actions
Using intimidation or authority to silence dissent
player_stance_axes.if_a_hardens: Factions respect the Warden's integrity but may act against them openly; legitimacy fractures more slowly; trust becomes a usable resource; the arc centers moral clarity and visible cost.
player_stance_axes.if_b_hardens: Factions suspect the Warden's motives; backroom deals create fragile, unstable coalitions; legitimacy erodes faster; betrayal becomes likely; the arc centers pragmatic survival and factional chess.

signals_a: Invoking the imperial writ and Throne authority
Prioritizing stability and institutional order
Framing the crisis as a failure of local leadership
Treating the settlement as subjects to be managed
Reporting fully and honestly to Throne superiors
signals_b: Identifying with the settlement's position
Framing the crisis as institutional failure
Treating locals as peers or allies
Prioritizing local survival over imperial order
Exceeding or bending the writ's authority
player_stance_axes.if_a_hardens: The Warden is seen as an outsider enforcing distant authority; Synod and House elites offer support; popular legitimacy erodes; the arc moves toward enforced order or institutional escalation.
player_stance_axes.if_b_hardens: The Warden is seen as a defector or frontier champion; locals offer trust and cooperation; Synod and House authority become adversaries; the arc moves toward revolt, extraction, or founding legitimacy outside the system.

signals_a: Refusing deals that violate sworn duty
Holding others to oaths and formal commitments
Treating the writ as inviolable
Refusing to falsify or omit in reports
Acting as a moral anchor even at personal cost
signals_b: Cutting deals that bend or break the writ
Treating oaths as negotiable under pressure
Prioritizing outcomes over principles
Falsifying records or reports to buy time or protect people
Using authority instrumentally rather than honorably
player_stance_axes.if_a_hardens: The Warden becomes a moral symbol; factions seek their witness and judgment; oaths become binding and powerful; the arc centers honor, cost, and the limits of duty.
player_stance_axes.if_b_hardens: The Warden becomes a pragmatic operator; factions seek deals and leverage; oaths lose weight; the arc centers survival, betrayal, and the erosion of principle under pressure.

chapter_function_map.function: Introduce the public crisis and map the factional terrain. The Warden arrives and must quickly assess which factions are mobilizing, what they want, and what leverage the Warden's authority actually holds. The settlement is already fracturing; the Warden's first actions signal which pressures they will answer.
chapter_function_map.pressure_question: Can the Warden assert control and map the factions before violence or open defiance begins?
possible_end_states: The Warden establishes authority and opens lines to all major factions.
The Warden aligns visibly with one faction, accelerating the crisis.
The Warden's authority is contested immediately, forcing early escalation.
The Warden discovers a hidden pressure (Undrift, House corruption, or militia fracture) that reframes the crisis.

chapter_function_map.function: Test the Warden's stance under pressure. Factions make demands, offers, or threats. The tithe clock advances. The Warden must act—enforce, protect, negotiate, or maneuver—and those actions will harden their position on at least one stance axis. The cost of inaction becomes visible.
chapter_function_map.pressure_question: What will the Warden sacrifice or risk to buy time, legitimacy, or protection for one faction over another?
possible_end_states: The Warden enforces compliance, earning Synod support but fracturing popular legitimacy.
The Warden protects families, earning popular trust but escalating Synod enforcement demands.
The Warden brokers a fragile compromise that satisfies no one but buys time.
The Warden is forced into visible violence or betrayal, accelerating factional collapse.

chapter_function_map.function: Escalate the crisis as pressure engines collide. Multiple factions act simultaneously. The Warden cannot satisfy everyone and must choose which pressures to resist and which to enable. Hidden agendas surface. The tithe clock is near. The cost of the Warden's earlier stances becomes undeniable.
chapter_function_map.pressure_question: Who will the Warden betray, abandon, or stand with when the factions force a choice?
possible_end_states: The Warden sides openly with the Synod and enforces selection, triggering revolt or submission.
The Warden sides openly with the Parents' Movement and becomes a defector or rebel.
The Warden brokers a desperate deal with a rival House or Undrift network, solving the crisis but poisoning future legitimacy.
The Warden fractures under the pressure and loses control as factions act independently.

chapter_function_map.function: Resolve or irreversibly commit to one path. The tithe ship is days away. The Warden's stance has hardened. Factions have mobilized. The crisis reaches its peak: enforcement, extraction, revolt, or collapse. The Warden must act decisively, knowing that their choice will define what this settlement becomes and what their authority means.
chapter_function_map.pressure_question: What will the Warden become in order to end this?
possible_end_states: The Warden enforces the tithe, delivering children to the Synod and preserving imperial order at moral cost.
The Warden enables extraction or falsification, betraying the Throne but protecting the settlement.
The Warden witnesses open revolt and must choose whether to suppress it, join it, or stand aside.
The Warden brokers a pyrrhic compromise that satisfies the letter of the mandate but hollows its spirit.
The Warden's authority collapses entirely, and the settlement descends into violence or flight.

chapter_function_map.function: Deliver the aftermath and long-term consequences. The tithe ship arrives or is avoided. The Warden must face the institutions and people they served, betrayed, or protected. The arc closes with the weight of what was chosen and what remains—loyalty, legitimacy, survival, or ruin.
chapter_function_map.pressure_question: What remains of the Warden's authority, integrity, and the settlement after the crisis?
possible_end_states: The Warden reports to the Throne, facing judgment or reward for their actions.
The Warden defects or is branded a traitor, leaving imperial service.
The settlement endures under new terms—protected, enslaved, or broken.
The Warden becomes a symbol of resistance or enforcement, with their legend spreading across the frontier.
The Warden is left alone with the cost of what they did and who they became.

possible_endgames: The tithe is met through enforcement; the Warden preserves imperial order but is haunted by the children taken.
The tithe is evaded through extraction; families escape, and the Warden becomes a defector or Undrift ally.
The settlement revolts openly; the Warden suppresses it, joins it, or witnesses its destruction.
A rival House claims the settlement; the tithe is met, but loyalty has fractured permanently.
The Warden brokers a compromise that satisfies the mandate on paper but subverts it in practice.
The Warden's authority collapses; the settlement fragments, and the frontier becomes ungovernable.
The Warden dies or is martyred in the crisis, becoming a symbol whose meaning is contested by survivors.
The tithe ship arrives to find compliance—and silence. The Warden has ensured order, but trust is gone.
